Context 6 / 29
MAY 15, 2026 · GOOGLE SEARCH CENTRAL

AEO and SEO are one strategy.
Google just made it official.

Their new AI optimization guide confirms the exact framework we’re covering today.

01
RAG is how Google AI works
Google uses retrieval-augmented generation across all AI features. The same mechanism we map across your four surfaces today.
“A technique used to improve quality, accuracy, and freshness of AI responses by relying on our core Search ranking systems.”
02
Website and technical structure come first
Google’s guide opens with foundational SEO: crawlability, technical clarity, unique content. The website surface is non-negotiable.
“Apply SEO best practices to generative AI search: building a clear technical structure and creating unique, valuable content.”
03
AEO and GEO are the right terms
Google officially acknowledges AEO and GEO as the terms for this work, and confirms it’s all still SEO. The foundation is the same.
“These are terms you may see used to describe work specifically focused on improving visibility in AI search experiences.”
developers.google.com/search/docs/fundamentals/ai-optimization-guide · Published May 15, 2026
GrowthX · checkthat.ai 7 / 29
How AI search actually works

Search → Retrieve → Synthesize
This is why four surfaces matter, not one.

11 / 29
Surface 01

Your Website

Where AI starts, and where SEO makes AEO possible

What to do
Produce fresh, structured content — regularly. Add FAQ schema to your top 3 pages. Rewrite your About page so AI can extract your positioning in 2–3 sentences. Make sure your homepage explicitly states what you do, who you serve, and what makes you different.
76%
of AI Overview citations come from Google's top 10 results. SEO is the foundation AEO is built on.
Ahrefs · AI Citation Research
What good looks like
AI describes your brand accurately in 2–3 sentences, citing your own pages. Your positioning language shows up in the response.
12 / 29
Surface 02

Third-Party Mentions

Where Perplexity, Claude, and Gemini are actually looking

What to do
Identify the 3 directories your ICP uses most — G2, Capterra, Product Hunt — and make sure you're accurately listed. Pitch guest articles or data studies to publications in your category. Brand mentions matter more than backlinks.
78%
of AI citations come from third-party authoritative publications, not brand-owned content.
GeoBuddy · 50,000 AI responses
What good looks like
Multiple third-party sources describe your brand consistently, using language close to your own positioning.
13 / 29
Surface 03

Community & Reviews

The signal AI trusts because it didn't come from you

What to do
Pick one community where your ICP is most active and show up consistently. Long-form Reddit comments (300–600 words, 50+ upvotes) get prioritized by LLMs. Claim and keep your G2 and Capterra profiles current.
46.7%
of Perplexity citations come from Reddit — the largest single source for B2B AI answers.
Red Engage · 2026 study
What good looks like
Real users describing your product in language that matches your positioning. Positive sentiment, specific use cases.
14 / 29
Surface 04

PR & Earned Media

The signal that tells AI you're a real, established brand

What to do
Identify 2–3 podcasts or publications your ICP reads. Pitch a guest spot or byline — editorial placement, not press releases. Make sure coverage is not paywalled. One strong earned story can become the only AI source for a query.
82–89%
of AI citations across all major LLMs come from earned media — consistent across studies.
Generative Pulse · May 2026
What good looks like
3–5 credible media mentions in the past 12 months. At least one podcast or guest article. Coverage is not paywalled.

CheckThat: run your AI visibility audit

Track what AI says about your brand across every engine, every day. Built by GrowthX.

01
Go to checkthat.ai Free access to 1.6M+ AI answers per month.
02
Search your brand or category 201 B2B categories mapped. See your score vs. competitors across ChatGPT, Claude, Perplexity, Google AI.
03
Read your visibility score 55% = you appear in 55% of relevant queries. See which engines you're winning vs. losing.
04
Identify your surface gaps Low score = highest-leverage action. Use the 4-surface framework to prioritize.
05
Track changes over time Watch your visibility move as you work the surfaces. Historical tracking across all engines.
20 / 29
CHECKTHAT SCORE GUIDE

What to do when a surface score is low

Part 1 of 2
Website
Add FAQ schema to your top 3 pages. Rewrite your About page with a clear, structured company description. Make sure your homepage explicitly states what you do, who you serve, and what makes you different.
Audit prompt
"What does [brand] do? Who do they serve? What makes them different from competitors?"
Run in ChatGPT. Note what it says and what it sources.
Third-party mentions
Identify the 3 directories your ICP uses most (G2, Capterra, Product Hunt, etc.) and get listed accurately. Then find 2 industry publications or newsletters and pitch a guest contribution or get featured.
Audit prompt
"What is [brand]? What third-party sources do you have information from about them?"
Run in Perplexity. Note every source it pulls.
GrowthX · checkthat.ai 21 / 29
CHECKTHAT SCORE GUIDE

What to do when a surface score is low

Part 2 of 2
Community + Reviews
Pick one community where your ICP is most active (Reddit, Slack, LinkedIn, Circle) and show up consistently, answer questions, share insights, be findable. Proactively ask happy customers for G2 or Capterra reviews.
Audit prompt
Search Reddit and LinkedIn for your brand name. Note the language real users use to describe you.
Also check your G2 / Capterra review themes.
PR Coverage
Identify 2–3 podcasts or publications your ICP reads and pitch a guest spot or byline. If you have existing coverage, check whether it’s AI-readable: not paywalled, clearly structured, with your brand name prominent.
Audit prompt
"Has [brand] been covered in the press or mentioned in any publications recently?"
Run in Gemini. Note what surfaces and whether it's AI-readable.

EXTERNAL VALIDATION

Everything we covered today is what Google recommends.

Official guide from Google Search Central. Published May 15, 2026.

Google says
What this session covered
“Create unique, non-commodity content. A first-hand review provides a unique perspective, whereas a summary of existing content simply restates information already available elsewhere.”
Website surface Your content strategy and unique POV is what AI cites and quotes
“Build and maintain a clear technical structure. Technical clarity ensures your content is ready for discovery and indexing.”
Citation surface Third-party mentions, structured data, and technical signals AI engines rely on
“Optimize your local business and ecommerce details. Business profiles can help your products and services appear in AI responses.”
Knowledge Panel surface Authority signals, business data, and Google profile completeness
“AEO and GEO are terms used to describe work focused on improving visibility in AI search experiences. From Google’s perspective, this is still SEO.”
AI Platform surface CheckThat tracks exactly this, every day, across 201 B2B categories
developers.google.com/search/docs/fundamentals/ai-optimization-guide · May 15, 2026
GrowthX · checkthat.ai 24 / 29

Step by step

Audit and establish baseline

  1. Inputs + get context right
  2. Map and prioritize clusters of opportunities
  3. Calibrate content quality
  4. Fix any blocking website issues
  5. Build and adapt agentic workflows
  6. Ramp up publishing
  7. Use signals to guide ongoing

Audit and establish baseline

  1. Get context right
  2. Map your ecosystem and buying categories
  3. Map and rank every player
  4. Map buyer questions (evaluation stage)
  5. Measure and establish AI visibility baseline
  6. Full website audit → health, content, structure, SEO/AEO
  7. Connect analytics and start measuring

The 4-Surface AI Visibility Audit

Run this for your brand after the session. Four surfaces, one row each.

Surface
What AI currently sees about you
Visible gap
One action this week
Website
Third-party mentions
Community / reviews
PR coverage
The prompts Marcel used live are on the next slide →
Fill this in for your brand26 / 29
Your audit toolkit

Four prompts. Run them tonight.

Surface 01: Your Website
Ask ChatGPT:
"What does [your brand] do?"
Note what it says and what it cites. Does your own site show up?
Surface 02: Third-Party Mentions
Ask Perplexity:
"What is [your brand]?"
Note every source it pulls from. How many are sites you don't own?
Surface 03: Community & Reviews
Search Reddit for your brand name.
Check G2 or Capterra.
Do the themes match your positioning? What are practitioners actually saying?
Surface 04: PR & Earned Media
Ask Gemini:
"Has [your brand] been covered in the press?"
Is your existing coverage AI-readable? Not paywalled, brand name prominent.
